steps.title: Find the key signal
steps.content: The passage opens with the narrator describing how “my path… had wound about and about so confusedly… that I no longer knew in what direction” to proceed. This phrase directly signals confusion and disorientation about the route, pinpointing the central challenge he faces during the trip.

steps.title: Match the meaning
steps.content: Choice A captures the narrator’s admission of being lost in the confusing terrain. The other three choices either add elements not mentioned in the text (e.g., fond memories in B, overcoming struggles in D, or familiarity with the location in C) or misframe the main idea entirely. That matches choice A — A is correct.
